name: zero-trust-verification
description: Zero-Trust Verification: a software-quality system for the AI-assisted development lifecycle, shipped as ONE plugin (ADR 0025 — consolidation of the former six-plugin suite). Spec generation (shift-left interrogation), the autopilot implementation drain, the codebase-health audit + PR gate, the Merge Marshal coordination backstop, Org-Wide Memory (a read-only, refuse-by-default index over repo-resident memory), and Production-Telemetry Triage (a read-only, bounded-window source that turns an emitted production incident into a resumable incident-Spec) — integrated through a shared machine-readable Verification Manifest (ADR 0001; plugin boundary collapsed by ADR 0025). This root marketplace is the product entry point; the plugin pins schema_version and degrades gracefully when the manifest is absent.

plugins.name: zero-trust
plugins.description: The whole Zero-Trust Verification suite in one plugin: /spec (interrogate raw intent into a Spec + complete Verification Manifest, refuse-to-finalize, human-only confirmed-CORE), /autopilot (autonomous TDD drain into PR-per-Story with the D6.5 mutation gate and N=5 determinism, host-agnostic GitHub + Bitbucket DC), /audit + /verify + /remediate + /health-loop (closed-loop codebase health: deterministic evidence, agent judgment, closing tests rerun 5x, debt ratchet), /marshal-pass + /marshal-staleness (serial deterministic composed-state merge backstop — post-rebase build verification, zero agent judgment in the merge path), org-memory (read-only refuse-by-default MCP index over repo-resident memory), and /triage (production incident -> resumable incident-Spec through one vendor-neutral telemetry adapter). One copy of every vendored artifact lives inside the plugin.
